overview.description: Chakrashila Wildlife Sanctuary originally Sikhri-Sikhla Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary falling under Kokrajhar district and some adjacent areas of Dhubri district of Assam, India. It is famous for the golden langur and is the second protected habitat for golden langurs in India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chakrashila Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chakrashila Wildlife Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Chakrashila Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Lokpriya Gopinath Bordoloi International Airport, Guwahati (128 km) and the nearest railway station is Alipurduar Junction (82 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Chakrashila Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Chakrashila Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Chakrashila Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Chakrashila Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.
